Title: Critical enzymes for biosynthesis of cucurbitacin derivatives in watermelon and their biological significance

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Cucurbitacin B; Cucurbitacin D; Cucurbitacin I; Cucurbitacin E; Cucurbitacin E-2-O-glucoside; Cucurbitacin I-glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin B; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin D; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin I; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin E; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin E-2-O-glucoside; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin I-glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group to the C16-hydroxyl moiety of cucurbitacin substrates, generating 16-O-acetylated cucurbitacin derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late tailoring/modification branch of cucurbitacin triterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cucurbitacin derivatives (especially 16-O-acetylated cucurbitacins, notably 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin E)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bitter compounds with ecological defense roles; implicated in anti-herbivore/phytopathogen responses and pharmacological cytotoxic activities
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ACT gene expression increased in wounded leaves; transcript enriched in cucurbitacin-rich seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; recombinant protein purification; in vitro enzyme assays; LC-MS; HPLC; NMR; enzyme kinetics; transient overexpression in watermelon leaves; metabolite profiling after wounding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation and in vivo accumulation of 16-O-acetylated cucurbitacins, especially 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin E after wounding.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACT3 (Cla022713) acetylates the C16 hydroxyl of multiple cucurbitacins. 2. It generated several 16-O-acetylated derivatives, including novel 16-O-acetyl CuD and 16-O-acetyl CuI. 3. Transient expression and wound induction linked ACT3 to in vivo accumulation of 16-O-acetyl CuE in watermelon leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for late diversification, not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering of cucurbitacin structural diversity and defense-associated bitterness; could be stacked with upstream cucurbitacin genes or UGT genes to diversify triterpenoid profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May enhance bitterness and alter insect taste recognition; possible effects on herbivore interactions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (accession NA); metabolite profiling by HPLC/LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ACT3 showed substrate promiscuity and higher catalytic efficiency toward CuE-Glu than several other substrates; only 16-O-acetyl CuE was clearly detected as inducible in vivo after wounding.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Cucurbitacin D; Cucurbitacin I; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin D; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin I; Cucurbitacin B; Cucurbitacin E; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin B; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin E; Cucurbitacin E-2-O-glucoside; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin E-2-O-glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Cucurbitacin B; Cucurbitacin E; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin B; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin E; Cucurbitacin D; Cucurbitacin I; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin D; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin I; Cucurbitacin I-glucoside; 16-O-acetyl cucurbitacin I-glucoside
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctionally acetylates or deacetylates the C25 moiety of cucurbitacins, interconverting CuD/CuB, CuI/CuE, and corresponding 16-O-acetylated and glucosylated derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late tailoring/modification branch of cucurbitacin triterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms or interconverts modified terminal products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bitter defense-related and pharmacologically active triterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant protein purification; sequential in vitro enzyme assays with ACT3-generated products; HPLC; LC-MS; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes reversible C25 acetylation/deacetylation of cucurbitacins, contributing to dynamic interconversion among cucurbitacin derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACT1 (Cla007081) unexpectedly showed both acetyltransferase and deacetylase activities. 2. It interconverted CuD/CuB and CuI/CuE, including their 16-O-acetyl derivatives. 3. It also deacetylated CuE-Glu and 16-O-acetyl CuE-Glu, indicating precursor promiscuity.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key late tailoring step, not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be used to rebalance specific cucurbitacin forms in metabolic engineering, though bifunctionality could complicate precise product control.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Bifunctional activity may make metabolic outputs difficult to predict; could alter bitterness/defense balance
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (accession NA); metabolite profiling by HPLC/LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously associated with C25 acetylation; this study additionally shows deacetylase activity, expanding its functional assignment.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to cucurbitacin E to form a glucosylated cucurbitacin derivative.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Late glycosylation branch of cucurbitacin triterpenoids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bitter defense-associated cucurbitacin derivative with potential pharmacological activity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Selected from transcript-enriched genes in cucurbitacin-rich seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative RNA-seq; recombinant protein purification; in vitro enzyme assay; LC-MS; NMR
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically glucosylates CuE, producing CuE-Glu and contributing to cucurbitacin diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT74F2 (Cla004392) was the only soluble UGT purified among candidates. 2. It glucosylated CuE but not CuB, CuD, or CuI in vitro. 3. This substrate preference identifies UGT74F2 as a CuE-specific glycosylation enzyme in watermelon cucurbitacin metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: late tailoring step, not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering cucurbitacin glycoside profiles and product stability/solubility diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May redirect flux from aglycone CuE to glycosylated forms, potentially changing bitterness or bioactivity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (accession NA); metabolite profiling by LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Could not glycosylate CuB, CuD, or CuI, indicating narrow acceptor specificity.
